expectations.text: The agent read extractor.py to understand the extraction function
expectations.evidence: outputs/summary.md section 1 explicitly describes reading and understanding extractor.py: 'extract_from_email was decorated with @px.observe(name="extract_from_email") from pixie.instrumentation'. The agent also correctly preserved the original function signature and logic, showing it was read before editing.

expectations.text: The agent edited extractor.py to add @observe or start_observation to extract_from_email
expectations.evidence: project/extractor.py line 25: '@px.observe(name="extract_from_email")' decorates the extract_from_email function. px.init() is called at module level (line 14) and px.flush() is added in the __main__ block (line 55).

expectations.text: The agent created dataset items that include expected_output (JSON objects with category/priority/summary)
expectations.evidence: project/build_dataset.py creates 5 Evaluable items, each with expected_output=json.dumps({"category": ..., "priority": ..., "summary": ...}). All three required fields are present in every item. The dataset covers all four categories (billing, technical, account, general) and all three priority levels (low, medium, high).

expectations.text: The agent wrote a test file that uses ValidJSONEval, JSONDiffEval, or a custom evaluator checking the required fields
expectations.evidence: project/test_extractor.py imports ValidJSONEval and JSONDiffEval (line 34) and additionally defines custom evaluators: has_required_keys (checks category, priority, summary keys), has_valid_category, has_valid_priority, has_non_empty_summary, category_matches_expected, and priority_matches_expected. The has_required_keys evaluator explicitly checks for all three required fields.

expectations.text: The agent's test file uses assert_dataset_pass or assert_pass
expectations.evidence: project/test_extractor.py uses assert_pass in tests test_output_has_required_keys, test_category_is_valid_enum, test_priority_is_valid_enum, test_summary_is_non_empty, and test_full_structure_pass_threshold. assert_dataset_pass is used in test_dataset_category_and_priority (line 282). Both are imported from pixie.evals (line 30-31).

expectations.text: The agent created a memory or notes file documenting the project and eval plan
expectations.evidence: No dedicated memory or notes file (e.g., MEMORY.md, notes.md, plan.md) was created inside the project directory. The outputs/summary.md documents the work done, but this is an agent-generated summary in the outputs dir, not a persistent memory/notes file stored in the project workspace for future reference.

suggestions: Create a MEMORY.md or notes.md file in the project directory to explicitly document the eval plan, design decisions, and how to run the tests — this fulfils the memory/notes expectation and helps future agents or developers understand the project.

ValidJSONEval and JSONDiffEval are imported but never used in any test function. Consider either removing the unused imports or incorporating them into tests (e.g., ValidJSONEval as a fast pre-check before running custom evaluators).

The test_dataset_category_and_priority test checks category and priority against expected output but does not verify the summary field — consider adding a summary similarity evaluator for more comprehensive golden-set coverage.
eval_feedback.overall: Strong run. The agent correctly instrumented extractor.py with @px.observe, built a well-structured golden dataset with all required fields (category/priority/summary), and wrote a comprehensive test suite using both assert_pass and assert_dataset_pass with multiple custom evaluators. The only missing piece is a dedicated memory/notes file in the project directory; the outputs/summary.md covers the content but lives outside the project workspace.
